home.pricingSubtitle: Top up your balance and spend on any model. Funds do not expire.

pricing.title: Top Up
pricing.sub: with Russian card or Telegram Stars

faq.a: The aineron.ru balance is in rubles. Each message to an AI model costs a few rubles (usually 1–5 ₽). Funds do not expire, balance is topped up via Robokassa without commission.

faq.q: Is a VPN required to use the service?
faq.a: No. aineron.ru is a Russian service with servers in Russia. Accessible without VPN, payment with Russian cards, works in any browser and app.
Available models include GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Gemini 1.5 Pro, DeepSeek V3, Mistral, Llama, and over 200 others. For images: Stable Diffusion, Flux, Midjourney-compatible models.

faq.q: Is there an API for developers?
faq.a: Yes. The API is compatible with OpenAI SDK — just change the base_url and API key. Works with Cursor, VS Code (Continue), GitHub Copilot alternatives, and any OpenAI-compatible app.
Via Robokassa: Visa/MasterCard/Mir cards, YooMoney, SBP. Balance top-up for any amount and discounted subscriptions available. For legal entities — invoice and contract.

faq.q: What if the AI gives a poor response?
faq.a: Each message has a 'Regenerate' button — the AI will generate a new response without additional charge. If a technical error occurs, funds are automatically refunded.

home.finalText: 10 ₽ free. No VPN, no foreign cards.

projectInstructionsTab.description: Instructions apply to all chats in this project as a system prompt. The AI will follow them automatically with each response.
projectInstructionsTab.templateProgrammerText: You are an experienced programmer. Respond specifically and concisely, providing code examples. Preferred language is Python unless otherwise specified.
projectInstructionsTab.templateTranslatorText: You are a professional translator. Translate accurately and naturally, maintaining the original style. Provide translation options if necessary.
projectInstructionsTab.templateAnalystText: You are a data analyst. Structure responses using lists and tables. Base your answers on facts, avoiding assumptions without clarification.
projectInstructionsTab.templateEditorText: You are an experienced text editor. Improve clarity, style, and grammar. Briefly explain each change.
projectInstructionsTab.templateResearcherText: You are a researcher. Explore questions deeply, providing sources and diverse perspectives. Distinguish between established facts and hypotheses.
projectInstructionsTab.templateAssistantText: You are a polite and precise assistant. Respond in Russian. Clarify tasks if ambiguous. Structure responses for easy reading.

apiDocs.metaDescription: OpenAI and Anthropic-compatible API: GPT-5, Claude, Gemini, image and video generation, embeddings, audio, batch. Integration with Cursor, Cline, Continue. No VPN, from Russia.
apiDocs.title: API and Integrations
apiDocs.subtitle: OpenAI and Anthropic-compatible API for GPT-5, Claude, Gemini, and media generation — no VPN. Plus integrations with Cursor, Cline, Continue. Choose a section on the left.

intro.overviewLead: {brand} provides access to GPT-5, Claude, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, image and video generation via a standard OpenAI-compatible API — no VPN or foreign cards, directly from Russia. If your code already works with the OpenAI SDK, just change the base_url and key.
intro.overviewFormatsP: Two formats are supported: <b1>OpenAI</b1> (<ic1>/chat/completions</ic1>, <ic2>/images/generations</ic2>, <ic3>/embeddings</ic3>, <ic4>/audio/*</ic4>) and <b2>Anthropic</b2> (<ic5>/messages</ic5>). Streaming, batch processing, webhooks are available.
intro.gettingStartedItem1: Quickstart — create a key and make your first request.
intro.quickstartIntro: Three steps to your first response from the AI model.
intro.quickstartStep1: Create an API key in the dashboard — section API Keys. The key like ak_... is shown once, save it immediately.
intro.quickstartStep2: Set our base_url instead of api.openai.com in your SDK or HTTP client.
intro.quickstartStep3: Top up your balance and make requests — token-based billing is automatic.
intro.authIntro: All API requests require a key in the Authorization header.
intro.authItem1: Keys are created and deleted in the dashboard; you can have multiple (e.g., one for each tool).
intro.authItem2: The key is linked to your account and shared balance.
intro.authItem3: JWT tokens are supported for Mini App and internal integrations (issued upon authorization via Telegram).
intro.authWarnBody: Do not store <ic>ak_...</ic> in public repositories or client-side browser code. If the key is compromised — delete it in the dashboard and create a new one.
intro.billingIntro: Payment in rubles, charged per token. Transparent and predictable.
intro.billingItem1: Cost depends on the model and volume (input + output tokens).
intro.billingItem2: Balance and spending are visible in the dashboard analytics.
intro.billingItem3: Rate limit: up to 120 requests per minute per key (error 429 on exceed — pause/retry).
intro.billingItem4: If funds are insufficient, API returns <ic>402 insufficient_quota</ic>.
intro.billingTip: For IDE autocompletion, use a fast, cheap model (<ic>gpt-4o-mini</ic>), and for complex tasks, a powerful one. This saves costs and reduces latency.
intro.errorsIntro: Errors are returned in OpenAI-compatible format.

endpoints.chatIntro: Main endpoint for text models. Fully compatible with OpenAI, including streaming.
endpoints.chatModelSonnet: Best for code and long files
endpoints.chatCatalogCallout: Full current list — request GET /api/v1/models or in the catalog.
endpoints.messagesIntro: If using the official Anthropic SDK — just set our base_url.
endpoints.modelsIntro: Get the current list of available models.
endpoints.modelsCatalogP: A public catalog with categories and prices is also available:
endpoints.imagesIntro: Via the standard OpenAI Images API. Video is generated asynchronously.
endpoints.imagesVideoCallout: Video models (Sora 2, Veo 3.1, Kling) operate through the same interface, but results take 5–15 minutes — poll for generation status.
endpoints.embeddingsIntro: Text vector representations for search and RAG.
endpoints.batchIntro: Batch asynchronous processing of a large number of requests.
endpoints.batchP: Suitable for mass classification, annotation, generation — without manual rate limit control.

sandboxes.title: Sandboxes — Code Execution in microVM
sandboxes.lead: Isolated sandboxes for safe execution of untrusted code — written by AI agents or your users. Each sandbox is a separate Firecracker microVM with its own kernel, ready in seconds. The only such API in Russia: rubles, RF offer, one key with chats and generation.
sandboxes.typicalScenariosP: Typical scenarios: agent 'LLM writes code → sandbox executes', student code verification, snippet execution from your product, dev servers with public URL.
sandboxes.quickstartIntro: The key must have the 'sandboxes' scope — enabled when creating the key in the dashboard.
sandboxes.billingTipBody: Reserve is deducted upon creation (TTL × price), unused minutes are automatically refunded upon stop. Launch error — full refund.

sandboxesRef.title: Sandboxes API Reference
sandboxesRef.limit1: 3 concurrent sandboxes per user; 240 minutes per day.
sandboxesRef.limit2: exec: timeout up to 300 s, output up to 256 KB (flag <ic>truncated</ic>), 30 runs/min.
sandboxesRef.limit3: Files: up to 5 MB per file, up to 50 files per request; paths — under <ic1>/home/user</ic1>, <ic2>/tmp</ic2>, <ic3>/app</ic3>.
sandboxesRef.limit4: Network from inside sandbox: allowlist (pypi, npm, etc.); need a domain — contact support.
sandboxesRef.idempotencyBody: POST-create and DELETE accept the <ic>Idempotency-Key</ic> header — repeat with the same key returns the first result and does not create a duplicate (important for retries).

sandboxesAgent.title: AI Agent that Writes and Executes Code
sandboxesAgent.intro: Full cycle on a single aineron key: LLM generates a solution via /chat/completions, the sandbox safely executes it, the result is returned to the agent.
sandboxesAgent.patternP: The pattern expands to full tool use: describe the sandbox as a tool (<ic1>run_python</ic1>) in the <ic2>tools</ic2> parameter of the model request — and the model will decide when to execute code and when to respond with text.
sandboxesAgent.tipBody: Keep one sandbox per dialogue (not per call): state — variables, installed packages, files — is preserved between <ic>exec</ic>, and startup is not wasted repeatedly.

resources.resChatsIntro: Web functions are also available programmatically — you can build your own interfaces on top of the platform.
resources.resAgentTitle: Memory, AI Tasks, and Agent Mode (API)
resources.resResearchIntro: Initiate research and check status from your code.
resources.resWebhooksOutgoingIntro: Receive platform events at your URL (with HMAC signature).

ide.cursorIntro: AI editor based on VS Code. Supports custom OpenAI providers from version 0.40.
ide.cursorStep2: In section <b1></b1> enable <b2></b2> and enter <urlIc></urlIc>. In field <b3></b3> — your <keyIc></keyIc>.
ide.cursorStep3: In specify the model, e.g., or . List — by request:
ide.cursorStep4: → select model in the bottom right corner → chat . Done.
ide.cursorCalloutInfo: If funds are insufficient, autocomplete will temporarily disable — top up balance in the dashboard.
ide.clineIntro: VS Code agent extension: creates and edits files, executes commands.
ide.clineStep1: Install from the VS Code marketplace.
ide.clineStep3: Recommended models for agent tasks: <ic1></ic1> (best for code), <ic2></ic2>, <ic3></ic3>.
ide.clineStep4: Describe the task in Russian or English. Cline will ask for permission before each file change.
ide.clineCalloutWarn: Agent sessions read file context and consume more tokens than regular chat. Monitor your balance.
ide.continueIntro: Open AI assistant for VS Code and JetBrains. Configured via JSON file.
ide.continueStep3: Save — Continue will apply the configuration. Select model in the chat dropdown.
ide.continueRowCtrlL: Open chat / add selection to context

more.telegramGuideP: How to connect the bot — see user guide.
more.toolsPlaygroundBody: Interactive sandbox — try requests in the browser. Open →
more.toolsSwaggerBody: Full interactive schema of all endpoints. Open →
more.devFaqQ1: Which model to choose for code?
more.devFaqA2: Limit of 120 requests/min per key. IDEs usually retry automatically. If you hit the limit constantly — create multiple keys for different tools.
more.devFaqA3: Yes, pass <ic1></ic1> — as in OpenAI SDK. Supported wherever <ic2></ic2> is available.
more.devFaqQ4: How to check balance from code?
more.devFaqQ5: Is it compatible with LangChain / LlamaIndex?
more.devFaqA5: Yes — it's an OpenAI-compatible provider. Specify <ic></ic> and the key in the OpenAI client settings within the framework.
more.devFaqHelpBody: Support contacts are in the website footer. For user functions, see the user guide.

sandbox.metaTitle: Sandboxes — Code Sandboxes for AI Agents | {brand}
sandbox.metaDescription: API for isolated microVMs to safely run untrusted code: AI agents, EdTech, snippet execution. Starts in seconds, pay with crypto from {price}/min.
sandbox.metaKeywords: code sandbox api, sandbox api, code execution api, e2b alternative, code interpreter api, ai agent sandbox
sandbox.heroTitle: Code Sandboxes for Your AI Agents
sandbox.heroSubtitle: Isolated microVMs with a single API call: your agent writes code, the sandbox runs it safely. Start in seconds, pay with crypto — no bank card required.
sandbox.freeTrialNote: New users get a starting balance: enough for initial tests without a card
sandbox.featuresTitle: Everything You Need for Untrusted Code Execution
sandbox.feature1Text: Each sandbox is a separate Firecracker microVM with its own kernel. The agent's code won't reach your data or neighbors.
sandbox.feature2Text: Warm pool of virtual machines: p95 start time under 5 seconds. State is preserved between code runs in one session.
sandbox.feature3Text: Full set: file read/write, command and code execution (Python, Node, bash), background processes, log streaming.
sandbox.feature4Text: Run a web server inside the sandbox — get an HTTPS address accessible from a browser. Demos and previews without deployment.
sandbox.feature5Text: Top up with USDT or TON via Crypto Pay — no bank account, card, or subscription required. Works the same way across the whole platform.
sandbox.feature6Text: The same API key works with chats (GPT-5, Claude), image generation, and sandboxes. The agent is entirely on one platform.
sandbox.pricingNote: Unused minutes are refunded upon stopping. Launch error — full refund.
sandbox.faq1Q: How is this different from running code in Docker on your own?
sandbox.faq1A: Docker containers share the kernel with the host — escaping the container through a kernel vulnerability is possible. Our sandboxes are full-fledged microVMs with a separate kernel, like AWS Lambda. Plus, you don't need to maintain and secure your own servers.
sandbox.faq2A: {smallPrice}/min for small (1 vCPU / 1 GiB) and {standardPrice}/min for standard (2 vCPU / 2 GiB). Rounded up to the nearest minute. TTL cost is reserved at creation, unused minutes are refunded upon stopping. Launch error — full refund.
sandbox.faq3A: AI agents that write and execute code (code interpreter); student solution verification in EdTech; safe execution of user scripts in SaaS; quick demos of web applications with a public URL.
sandbox.faq4A: Yes, Python SDK: pip install aineron. For other languages — standard REST API with Bearer authorization, curl examples in the documentation.
sandbox.faq5A: Up to 3 concurrent Sandboxes and 240 minutes per day per account, TTL up to 60 minutes, command output up to 256 KB. Network access from inside is allowlist-based (pypi, npm, etc.); need your own domain — contact support.

docs.metaDescription: Complete guide to aineron: chats, image and video generation, projects with knowledge base, memory, Deep Research, AI agent, AI tasks, and Telegram bot. In simple terms.
docs.subtitle: Complete guide in simple terms: chats and generation, projects with knowledge base, memory, research, AI agent, and Telegram bot. Choose a section on the left.

intro.overviewLead: aineron provides access to all leading AI models worldwide from one interface — pay with crypto, no VPN required. GPT-5, Claude, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, image and video generation, plus smart extensions not found in regular 'AI chats': memory, knowledge base, research with sources, and an AI agent that performs tasks independently.
intro.overviewWaysP: There are three ways to use it, all operating on a single balance:
intro.overviewWebBody: Chat, model catalog, projects, image and video generation — directly in the browser.
intro.overviewBotBody: The same AI in a messenger: message the bot @aineron_bot. Can even work on a schedule.
intro.overviewApiBody: Connect to your applications and IDEs (Cursor, Cline, Continue). API Documentation →
intro.diffTitle: How We Differ from a Regular 'Chatbot'
intro.diffItem1: All Models at Once — not tied to a single vendor, switch between GPT, Claude, Gemini with one click.
intro.diffItem2: Memory — the platform remembers facts about you and your projects to avoid repetition.
intro.diffItem3: Projects with Knowledge Base — upload your files, and the AI responds based on them.
intro.diffItem4: Deep Research — multi-step research with real sources and citations.
intro.diffItem5: AI Agent — searches, calculates, reads your files, and suggests edits.
intro.diffItem6: Proactive Telegram Bot — sends morning briefs, tracks topics, responds to your clients.
intro.diffTipTitle: Tip for Beginners and Vibe Coders
intro.diffTipBody: No need to know the terms. Just start with the chat — ask a question in plain language. Other features will be useful when you want more: build a project, research a topic, or automate routine tasks.
intro.startLeadP: Create an account in a minute — and immediately receive a free balance to try.
intro.startStep1: Click “Start for Free” in the top right corner of the site.
intro.startStep2: Register via email and password or through a social network: Google, GitHub — in one click.
intro.startStep3: If registering via email, confirm your email: enter the 6-digit code from the email.
intro.startStep4: Done — you'll enter onboarding, and trial funds will already be on your balance.
intro.themeBody: The sun/moon icon in the header switches the theme: system → light → dark. The choice is saved in the browser. The entire platform, including this documentation, supports both themes.
intro.balanceLead: The balance is in credits. Each message to an AI model costs a little: from a fraction of a credit for economical models to several credits for the most powerful ones. It is deducted automatically, no setup needed.
intro.balanceItem1: The balance is always visible in the site header and in the Mini App bot.
intro.balanceItem2: The cost per message depends on the selected model — it is indicated in the catalog.
intro.balanceItem3: If image or video generation fails, the funds are automatically returned.
intro.balanceItem4: New users receive a welcome balance for trial.
intro.topupCardBody: Dashboard → Plans and Payments section. Pay with USDT or TON via Crypto Pay.
intro.topupStarsBody: Payment directly in the messenger in two taps, instant crediting. Command <ic>/balance</ic> in the bot.
intro.topupTariffsP: You can simply top up the balance as needed, or subscribe to a monthly plan — it provides more credits on the balance for a lower price (bonus up to +40%) and sometimes unlimited access to certain models. Plans: Start, Standard, Pro, Max, and a separate 'Business' (for AI secretary). More details in the payment section.
intro.topupPromoBody: Have a promo code? Enter it in the payment section. Invite a friend using your referral link — receive a bonus when they pay. Referral Program →

chat.chatIntro: The main tool. Ask a question — get an answer. Below is everything the chat can do.
chat.streamingBody: The answer is typed as it is generated, no need to wait for the entire response.
chat.webSearchBody: The 'Internet' toggle in the input field — the AI will add fresh facts from the web to the answer.
chat.attachmentsBody: Attach a photo, PDF, document, or archive — the AI will read and consider the content.
chat.voiceBody: Dictate your question by voice; the answer can be read back.
chat.historyItem1: All dialogues are saved on the left in the chat list — you can return at any time.
chat.historyItem2: Buttons under the answer: like/dislike (rating), repeat, edit request, delete, read aloud.
chat.historyItem3: Branching — create a dialogue branch from any message to explore a different path without losing the main one.
chat.historyItem4: Response Options — get several versions at once (brief / detailed / step-by-step) and choose the best.
chat.historyItem5: Search through the entire chat history — by words and meaning (see 'Memory of All Chats').
chat.newChatTip: Want a new dialogue from scratch — command <ic>/newchat</ic> in the bot or 'New Chat' button on the site. The context of the previous conversation does not interfere.
chat.modelsIntro: All available models are collected in the catalog. Each has a description, price per message, and purpose.
chat.modelsTabsP: Models are divided into three tabs:
chat.modelsRowTextExamples: GPT-5, GPT-4o, Claude Opus/Sonnet, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, Qwen
chat.modelsHowItem1: Fast and cheap — DeepSeek, GPT-4o-mini, Gemini Flash. For simple queries and drafts.
chat.modelsHowItem2: Versatile — GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet, Gemini Pro. Balance of quality and price.
chat.modelsHowItem3: Maximum quality — GPT-5, Claude Opus, Grok — for complex reasoning and code.
chat.modelsSwitchP: Switch models in chat with one click, or in the bot with the /models command. Catalog for review: All models →.
chat.imagesIntro: Describe the image in words — the AI model will draw it. Advanced image processing modes are also available.
chat.imagesItem1: Text → Image — simply describe what you want to see.
chat.imagesItem2: Editing (img2img, inpaint) — upload an image and describe changes; mask an area to replace only it.
chat.imagesItem3: Enhancement — upscale (increase clarity), background removal, variations, image description.
chat.imagesItem4: Prompt Enhancement — a button enhances your description for more detailed results.
chat.imagesFilesCallout: All generated images and videos are stored in <a1>My Files</a1> and <a2>Favorites</a2>. Download, retry with different settings, or share from there.
chat.imagesPaidWarn: Image and video generation is available on paid plans. If generation fails for technical reasons, funds are automatically refunded.
chat.videoIntro: Short clips from text descriptions or photos. Prepared in 5–15 minutes — results are sent to chat or bot.
chat.videoRowKlingFeatures: 5/10 sec, quality modes, sound (in pro), negative prompt
chat.videoItem1: Text → Video — describe the scene and camera movement.
chat.videoItem2: Photo → Video (animation) — upload an image, and it will come to life.
chat.videoTip: Video is the most 'heavy' generation. Describe not only the object but also the movement: 'slow camera flight over a city at sunset'.
chat.compareIntro: Ask one question to two or three models at once and compare the answers side by side.
chat.compareItem1: Convenient to choose which model best solves your specific task.
chat.compareItem2: Responses are generated in parallel and streamed simultaneously.
chat.compareItem3: You can vote for the best response, contributing to the overall Elo rating of models.
chat.promptsIntro: Pre-made request templates for common tasks, so you don't have to formulate from scratch.
chat.promptsItem1: Built-in prompts by category + your own saved ones.
chat.promptsItem2: Insert into the input field with one click, then edit as needed.
chat.personasIntro: A persona is a predefined 'character' and role for the assistant (e.g., 'strict editor' or 'friendly teacher').
chat.personasItem1: Select a persona, and the AI will respond in its style in all messages.
chat.personasItem2: You can create your own personas with custom instructions.

projects.projectsIntro: A project unites chats, knowledge base files, and instructions into one space. It's like a separate 'room' for a specific task, client, or product.
projects.cardFolderBody: All project dialogues in one place, not mixed with others.
projects.cardKbBody: Uploaded files that the AI uses to respond (see next section).
projects.cardInstructionsBody: Project system prompt — common style and rules for all chats inside.
projects.cardMemoryBody: Facts learned in the project are tied to it and do not 'leak' to others.
projects.projectsOpenP: Open: My Projects →. Each project has a color, icon, and tabs: Files, Instructions, Git/Sources, Team, Journal.
projects.kbIntro: Upload your files to the project, and the AI will respond based on them. This is called RAG: the AI model finds relevant fragments of your documents and uses them in the response.
projects.kbUploadP: PDF, Word, text, spreadsheets, presentations, archives, code. Files are automatically parsed and indexed.
projects.kbHowH3: How It Works Under the Hood (In Simple Terms)
projects.kbHowItem1: The platform finds the most relevant parts of your files by meaning (hybrid search + reranking — higher quality than regular word search).
projects.kbHowItem2: These parts are seamlessly added to your question, and the model responds based on them.
projects.kbHowItem3: Under the response, you can see which files were used (sources).
projects.kbCommandsH3: Manual Management — 'Commands' Directly in the Message
projects.kbCmdFileDesc: Retrieve a specific file entirely, bypassing search
projects.kbCmdWebDesc: Add fresh web search to the response
projects.kbCmdCodebaseDesc: Search the code of the connected repository
projects.kbDashboardTipBody: Tab with file status: what's indexed, number of fragments, 'Reindex' button. If a file is missing from responses, check its status there.
projects.connectorsIntro: The knowledge base can be populated not only by uploading files but also automatically from a repository, site, or news feed.
projects.connectorsGitBody: Connect a repository via token — code is synchronized into the knowledge base, AI understands your codebase. Updates are pulled automatically.
projects.connectorsSiteBody: Specify the site or documentation address — pages will be added to the knowledge base and rescanned daily.
projects.connectorsRssBody: New feed entries are added to the knowledge base daily — convenient for monitoring topics or news.
projects.connectorsHowP: Connect on the project page → Git/Sources tab → 'Connect' → select type. For site and RSS, no token is needed, just the address.
projects.codeIntro: For projects with a connected repository, an editor and change suggestions are available.
projects.codeItem1: Built-in code editor (JS/HTML/CSS highlighting) directly in the project.
projects.codeItem2: AI commits — AI can suggest file changes; you review and confirm them with a button (nothing changes without your consent). For large files, pinpoint patch editing is used.
projects.codeItem3: Pull Request or direct commit — your choice.
projects.codeItem4: Version history of each file with rollback capability.
projects.codeAgentCallout: AI agent (see 'Agent Mode' section) can read project files and suggest such edits — like a commit for confirmation.
projects.collabIntro: The project can be shared with colleagues.
projects.collabItem1: Invite collaborators with the role of <b1>viewer</b1> (read-only) or <b2>editor</b2> (edit).
projects.collabItem2: Activity log (audit) records who did what.
projects.collabItem3: The project can be published as a public Space — a read-only open page.
projects.graphIntro: A visual map of connections between files and concepts in your project.
projects.graphP: Shows how documents and topics are interconnected — useful for a quick overview of a large knowledge base. Opens from the project page.

smart.memoryIntro: The platform remembers important facts about you from conversations, so it doesn't have to ask again. For example: what language you code in, your name, what you do.
smart.memoryRowPersonalWhat: General facts about you — in all chats
smart.memoryRowProjectWhat: Facts learned in the project — only within it
smart.memoryRowTeamWhat: General organization facts — for all members
smart.memoryItem1: Memory fills automatically, but everything can be viewed, edited, pinned, or deleted.
smart.memoryItem2: When the bot remembers something, it quietly notifies you (“Remembered: …”).
smart.memoryItem3: Memory can be completely disabled in settings.
smart.recallIntro: Ask “do you remember what we decided about billing?” — and the platform will find the relevant old chat by meaning, even if you don't remember where it was.
smart.recallItem1: Triggers on phrases like “do you remember…”, “we discussed…”, “last time…”, “remind me…”.
smart.recallItem2: Chat history search works by words and meaning — finds what you need even if phrased differently.
smart.recallCallout: It's like an assistant's long memory: the more you use it, the more useful it becomes, as it remembers the context of past tasks.
smart.researchIntro: Not just an answer, but a real mini-research: the platform formulates several search queries, reads sources, and compiles a report with links and citations.
smart.researchStep1: Ask a question or research topic.
smart.researchStep3: Receive a structured report with numbered citations [1][2] and a list of sources + downloadable file.
smart.researchItem1: In the project, the report can be saved to the knowledge base — and subsequent answers will consider it (knowledge accumulates).
smart.researchItem2: In bot — command <ic>/research</ic> (fixed price, confirmed before launch).
smart.agentTitle: Agent Mode — an agent that acts
smart.agentIntro: A regular chat responds. An agent acts: plans steps and executes them with tools until the task is solved.
smart.agentWhatP: What the agent can do in one request:
smart.agentItem3: Read your knowledge base and project files.
smart.agentItem4: Suggest file edits — like a commit for approval (nothing changes without you).
smart.agentCalloutBody: “Compare prices of the top 5 graphics cards and calculate the cost of a farm of 8 units” — the agent will find prices, calculate, and send a report. In bot — command <ic>/agent</ic>.
smart.tasksTitle: AI Tasks — Work While You Sleep
smart.tasksIntro: Assign a task to the platform that will run on schedule, and the result will be sent to you in Telegram.
smart.tasksItem1: Morning Brief — news on your topics and exchange rates every morning.
smart.tasksItem2: Monitoring — track a topic/keyword and send a summary.
smart.tasksItem3: Monitoring with Research — comprehensive Deep Research on a topic every week, report accumulates in the project's knowledge base.
smart.tasksItem4: Recurring Prompt — for example, a post to a channel every Monday.
smart.tasksCreateP: Created in plain language: in the bot /task every morning at 8 send AI news and dollar rate — the platform will parse the phrase itself. Management — Dashboard → AI Tasks or /tasks in the bot.
smart.tasksCalloutBody: Each run is charged at the model's price. The limit of active tasks depends on the plan; if funds are insufficient, the task is paused with a notification.

account.accountIntro: All settings, statistics, and account management in one place.
account.rowAnalyticsDesc: Daily spending, top models, usage statistics
account.rowBillingDesc: Top up, buy a plan, payment history, promo codes
account.rowReferralDesc: Your referral link, invitees, bonuses, withdrawal
account.rowMemoryDesc: What the platform remembers about you (management)
account.rowTelegramDesc: Link Telegram account to the bot
account.rowOauthDesc: Applications you have granted access to
account.settingsIntro: Fine-tune assistant behavior — on the website in chat and in the bot with the /settings command.
account.settingsItem1: Default Model — which AI model responds without explicit selection.
account.settingsItem2: System Prompt — constant instruction for the assistant (tone, role, rules).
account.settingsItem3: Web Search — enable/disable adding fresh data from the internet.
account.settingsItem5: Streaming — display the response as it is generated.

teams.orgsIntro: For companies and teams: shared balance, members, and shared memory.
teams.orgsItem1: Shared Balance for the organization and accounts — centralized payment for the entire team.
teams.orgsItem2: Members and Roles — invite colleagues, assign permissions.
teams.orgsItem4: Team Shared Memory — facts like “our stack is Django”, “releases on Fridays” are visible to all members in all chats.
teams.orgsItem5: Telegram Groups can be connected to the organization: the bot works in the group, and expenses are deducted from the shared balance.
teams.orgsFooterP: Management — organization dashboard section. To connect a group in the bot, use the group registration command.

telegram.startIntro: The same AI and the same balance — directly in Telegram. The bot functions as a full-fledged assistant and even proactively.
telegram.startStep2: Link your aineron account (via Dashboard → Telegram or by the bot's prompt) — balance and history will be shared.
telegram.startStep3: Ask questions in plain text or use commands from the menu.
telegram.startMenuTipBody: Click “Menu” next to the input field — all commands are there. Below is what they do.
telegram.basicIntro: A full assistant in the messenger.
telegram.basicRowImg2videoAction: Animate a photo — from image to video
telegram.basicRowMemoryAction: What the bot remembers about you
telegram.basicFilesP: Attached photos and documents will also be read and considered in the response.
telegram.agenticIntro: What regular bots lack: the bot works on schedule, explores, and performs tasks.
telegram.agenticRowAgentAction: Agent Mode: search + calculations + knowledge base → report
telegram.agenticRowChannelAction: AI manages your Telegram channel: scheduled posts
telegram.agenticRowTopicsAction: Topic projects in private (separate context on a topic)
telegram.agenticCalloutBody: AI tasks — “an assistant that works while you sleep.” Morning brief, topic monitoring, weekly reports — everything arrives automatically.
telegram.businessIntro: The bot responds to your clients on your behalf. A ready tool for freelancers, shops, experts — anyone overwhelmed with messages.
telegram.businessItem1: “Drafts” Mode (safe) — for each client message, the bot prepares a response, and you approve with “Send / Edit / Ignore.” Nothing is sent without your consent.
telegram.businessItem2: “Autopilot” Mode — the bot answers typical questions (hours, prices, delivery) itself, and forwards complex ones to you.
telegram.businessItem3: Secretary's Knowledge Base — connect a project with pricing and FAQ, and responses will be based on your documents.
telegram.businessItem4: Morning Summary — “7 messages overnight: 3 answered, 2 awaiting you.”
telegram.businessFooterP: Setup — command /secretary. More about the product: AI Secretary page →. Telegram Business is required for the account owner.
telegram.moreBotsIntro: Create your own assistant bot in minutes.
telegram.moreBotsItem1: Command <ic>/mybot</ic> → choose a ready-made persona template (Store Support, Lawyer, Tutor, Copywriter) or describe your own.
telegram.moreBotsItem2: Your <ic>@your_bot</ic> responds to guests using your model and knowledge base.
telegram.moreBotsItem3: Guest messages are charged from your balance (with a daily limit).
telegram.moreSubsItem1: Subscriptions in Telegram Stars — subscribe directly in the messenger (/subscribe), automatic renewal, cancel in /balance.
telegram.moreSubsItem2: Affiliate Program — channels can promote the bot for a commission from Telegram.
telegram.moreGroupsIntro: The bot is also useful in group chats.
telegram.moreGroupsItem1: <ic>/summary</ic> — brief summary of the discussion for N hours.
telegram.moreGroupsItem3: <ic>/stat</ic> — participant expenses (for teams with org billing).
telegram.miniappIntro: A full-fledged mini-app right in the messenger, no App Store installation required.
telegram.miniappItem2: Full-screen mode and 'Add to Home Screen' button (icon on the phone).
telegram.miniappItem3: Share your generation in any chat with one tap, post to Stories.
telegram.miniappFooterP: Opens from the bot or via direct link <ic>t.me/aineron_bot/app</ic>.

help.faqVpnA: No VPN needed. Pay with crypto — no bank card required.
help.faqBillingA: For each message to the AI model — at the price of the selected model. Cheaper models cost a fraction of a credit, powerful ones — a few credits. The price is listed in the catalog.
help.faqGenFailQ: Why did the image/video generation fail?
help.faqGenFailA: Sometimes the provider returns an error. In this case, funds are automatically refunded — just try again or switch the model.
help.faqDataA: Client correspondence in the AI secretary is not stored (only a queue of drafts with auto-deletion). Memory can be viewed and deleted at any time. Tokens of connected repositories are stored encrypted.
help.faqDiffQ: How do the site and bot differ?
help.faqDiffA: They are one platform with a shared balance and history. The site is more convenient for projects, knowledge bases, and generation; the bot — for quick questions, voice, and proactive tasks.
help.faqApiQ: I'm a developer — is there an API?
help.faqCalloutBody: Contact support — details are in the site footer. We will help you resolve it.

rootMeta.description: Access to GPT-5, Claude, Gemini, and other AI models — no VPN required, pay with crypto. Chat, image and video generation, API for developers.
